---
title: "Compositional data analysis of the Gong & Sharma et al. dataset"
author: "Christian Halter"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(),'%e de %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  rmdformats::downcute:
    lightbox: true
    thumbnails: false
    self_contained: true
    gallery: true
    code_folding: show
  pkgdown:
    as_is: true
---

Using exploratory compositional data analysis on large healthy data set

paper: <https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2024.09.10.612119v1> accession: TBD

This script takes output of "<https://apps.allenimmunology.org/aifi/insights/dynamics-imm-health-age/downloads/>" see "Cell type frequency data"


# For additional sample metadata see: "<https://apps.allenimmunology.org/aifi/insights/dynamics-imm-health-age/downloads/>"

# General study description from authors:
```{r}
# Immune aging is a dynamic process driven by complex internal and external interactions. We examined the healthy human immune system across age using deep molecular profiling .

# To do so, we used our Human Immune Health Atlas to label peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) from a prospective, longitudinally sampled cohort of healthy young adults (n=49, 25-35 years) and older adults (n=47, 55-65 years) followed over the course of 2-3 years. This cohort was observed under homeostatic conditions, as well as prior to, and at 7 and 90 days following, annual influenza vaccinations, resulting in a scRNA-seq dataset with over 13.5 million PBMCs.

# Our study highlights that gradual, age-related alterations in the homeostatic transcriptional networks of immune cells lead to shifts in immune responsiveness as we age.
```

# Cell type data description
```{r}
# Cell type frequency data
# After labeling cell types, we tabulated the cell count for each of the 868 samples utilized in our study at each level of resolution in our Immune Health Atlas. Below, we provide these cell counts, the fraction of counts for each sample, and the centered log ratio (CLR) transformation of those fractions that we utilized for our analyses.

# In addition, we utilized the absolute lymphocyte counts (ALC) provided in our clinical blood count lab results for each sample (available below) to compute estimated cell type abundance based on normalization to ALC. For more details, see our Data Analysis Methods.
```

# Metadata columns description
```{r}
# cohort.cohortGuid: Cohort ID (BR1 or BR2)
# subject.subjectGuid: Subject ID
# subject.biologicalSex: Subject Sex (Female or Male)
# subject.cmv: Subject CMV Status (Negative or Positive)
# subject.bmi: Subject BMI (integer)
# subject.race: Subject race
# subject.ethnicity: Subject ethnicity
# subject.birthYear: Subject Birth Year
# subject.ageAtFirstDraw: Subject Age at earliest blood draw in study
# sample.sampleKitGuid: Sample Kit ID
# sample.visitName: Sample Visit Name
# sample.drawDate: Sample Draw Date (Year-Month)
# sample.subjectAgeAtDraw: Subject age at time of draw, based on year of Draw Date and Birth Year
# specimen.specimenGuid: Specimen ID (pbmc_sample_id in .h5 files)

# Frequency-related columns
# (for AIFI_L1 as an example; AIFI_L1 is replaced with AIFI_L2 and AIFI_L3 for those levels)

# AIFI_L1: Cell Type assignment
# AIFI_L1_count: Count of cells within this sample with cell type assignment
# total_cells: Total cells within this sample
# scrna.lymphocyte_count: Sum of T, NK, and B cells
# bc.lymphocyte_count: Absolute Lymphocyte Count (ALC) from clinical Blood Counts (bc.)
# alc_ratio: ALC per scRNA Lymphocyte Count
# AIFI_L1_frac_total: Fraction of cells with cell type assignment divided by Total cells for this sample
# AIFI_L1_alc: ALC estimate for this cell type assignment
# AIFI_L1_clr: Centered Log Ratio computed using AIFI_L1_frac_total for all types within this sample
```



# Load libraries

```{r, include=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
# renv::init()
# renv::snapshot()

library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(forcats)
library(patchwork)
library(factoextra)
library(compositions)
library(pROC)
library(mclust)
library(Rfast)
library(zoo)
library(pheatmap)
source("functions.R")

library(Seurat)
# devtools::install_github('satijalab/seurat-data')
library(SeuratData)
# install.packages("hdf5r")
# remotes::install_github("mojaveazure/seurat-disk")
library(SeuratDisk)

# install.packages("devtools")
# devtools::install_github("GfellerLab/EPIC", build_vignettes=TRUE)
library(EPIC)
# BiocManager::install("MOFA2")
library(reticulate)
library(basilisk)
library("MOFA2")
# BiocManager::install("fgsea")
# BiocManager::install("sva")
# devtools::install_github("kharchenkolab/scITD")
library(scITD)
# install.packages("ncdf4")
library(ncdf4)
library(arrow)

library(dplyr)

my_seed <- 123
set.seed(my_seed)
```

# Set paths

```{r}
ds_name <- "GongSharma_2024_PrePrintTBD"

path_root <- file.path("", "Volumes", "Shared", "DataCollections", "Standardized_SingleCell_Datasets", ds_name)
path_data <- file.path(path_root, "data")
path_plots <- file.path(getwd(), "plots")
```


# Load dataset
Manually downloaded from:
"<https://apps.allenimmunology.org/aifi/insights/dynamics-imm-health-age/downloads/>"

```{r}
files <- list(L1 = file.path(path_data, "sound_life_AIFI_L1_frequencies.csv"),
              L2 = file.path(path_data, "sound_life_AIFI_L2_frequencies.csv"),
              L3 = file.path(path_data, "sound_life_AIFI_L3_frequencies.csv"))

data <- list()

for (f in names(files)) {
  data[[f]] <- read.csv(files[[f]])
}
```


# Cell types per layer

```{r}
t1 <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(table(data$L3$AIFI_L1, data$L3$AIFI_L2)))

for (i in ncol(t1):1) {
  t1 <- t1[order(t1[, i], decreasing = TRUE), ]
}

colnames(t1) <- str_replace_all(colnames(t1), " ", "_")
row.names(t1) <- str_replace_all(row.names(t1), " ", "_")

t2 <- as.data.frame.matrix(t(table(data$L3$AIFI_L2, data$L3$AIFI_L3)))

for (i in ncol(t2):1) {
  t2 <- t2[order(t2[, i], decreasing = TRUE), ]
}

colnames(t2) <- str_replace_all(colnames(t2), " ", "_")
row.names(t2) <- str_replace_all(row.names(t2), " ", "_")
```


# Plot number of cells per sample

```{r}
for (layer in names(data)) {
  df <- data[[layer]] %>%
    dplyr::select(paste0("AIFI_", layer),
           paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_frac_total"),
           paste0("total_cells"),
           "specimen.specimenGuid") %>%
    group_by(specimen.specimenGuid) %>%
    reframe(cells_sum = min(total_cells),
            annotated_cells_perc = sum(!!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_frac_total"))))
  
  p <- ggplot(data = df, aes(x = reorder(specimen.specimenGuid, cells_sum), y = cells_sum)) +
    geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="steelblue") +
    xlab("") +
    ylab("Cells") +
    theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 10)) +
    ggtitle(paste("Cell counts per sample - Layer: ", layer))
  print(p)
  
  p <- ggplot(data = df, aes(x = reorder(specimen.specimenGuid, cells_sum), y = annotated_cells_perc)) +
    geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="steelblue") +
    xlab("") +
    ylab("Cells") +
    theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks(n = 10)) +
    ggtitle(paste("Percent annotated cells - Layer: ", layer))
  print(p)
}
```

# Process data into wide dataframe

```{r}
dproc <- list()

for (layer in names(data)) {

  dproc$meta <- data[[layer]] %>%
    dplyr::select(c(1:14)) %>%
    unique() %>% 
    mutate_at(1:14, as.factor)
  
  ctable <- data[[layer]] %>%
    dplyr::select(c(14,
             !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer)),
             !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_count")),
             !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_frac_total")),
             !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_clr")))) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer)),
                values_from = c(!!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_count")),
                                !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_frac_total")),
                                !!sym(paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_clr")))) %>% 
    column_to_rownames(var = "specimen.specimenGuid")
  
  i <- 1
  n_cts <- length(unique(data[[layer]][[paste0("AIFI_", layer)]]))
  
  dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts <- ctable[, i:(i + n_cts - 1)] %>%
    mutate_all(~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
    rename_all(~ str_replace_all(., paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_count_"), "") %>%
                 str_replace_all(., " ", "_")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-Platelet, -Erythrocyte)

  i <- i + n_cts
  dproc[[layer]]$freq <- ctable[, i:(i + n_cts - 1)] %>%
    mutate_all(~replace_na(., 0)) %>%
    rename_all(~ str_replace_all(., paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_frac_total_"), "") %>%
                 str_replace_all(., " ", "_")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-Platelet, -Erythrocyte)
  i <- i + n_cts
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_authors <- ctable[, i:(i + n_cts - 1)] %>%
    rename_all(~ str_replace_all(., paste0("AIFI_", layer, "_clr_"), "") %>%
                 str_replace_all(., " ", "_")) %>%
    dplyr::select(-Platelet, -Erythrocyte)
  
  df_counts <- dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts

  df_counts_1count_zero <- df_counts
  df_counts_1count_zero[df_counts_1count_zero == 0] <- 1
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_zero <- df_counts_1count_zero %>% calc_perc_df() %>% clr()
  
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_all <- (df_counts + 1) %>% calc_perc_df() %>% clr()
  
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count <- df_counts %>%
    impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 1) %>%
    calc_perc_df() %>%
    clr()
  
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_0.1perc <- df_counts %>%
    impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "percentage_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.1) %>%
    calc_perc_df() %>%
    clr()
  
  dproc[[layer]]$clr_0.01perc <- df_counts %>%
    impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "percentage_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.01) %>%
    calc_perc_df() %>%
    clr()
}

dproc$meta$age <- factor(ifelse(dproc$meta$cohort.cohortGuid == "BR1", "Young", "Old"), levels = c("Young", "Old"))
dproc$meta$combined_cmv_age <- factor(paste0("CMV", ifelse(as.character(dproc$meta$subject.cmv) == "Positive", "+", "-"), " ", as.character(dproc$meta$age)),
                                      levels = c("CMV- Old",
                                                 "CMV+ Old",
                                                 "CMV- Young",
                                                 "CMV+ Young")
)

saveRDS(dproc, file.path(path_data, "cell_type_compositions.rds"))
```


# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Figure 1 A

## UMAP example

```{r}
source("Processed_dataset_metadata.R")

ds <- "Zhang"
seurat <- readRDS(file.path(file.path(getwd(), "data"), paste0(datasets[[ds]][["ds_name"]], seurat_file_ending)))
seurat_sub <- subset(seurat, downsample = 500)

seurat_sub <- RunUMAP(seurat_sub, dims = 1:10)
Idents(seurat_sub) <- "RNA_snn_res.0.1"
DimPlot(seurat_sub, reduction = "umap") + theme_void() & NoLegend()
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_1_A_umap.pdf"),
       width = 4,
       height = 4)

df <- table(seurat_sub$Sample, seurat_sub$RNA_snn_res.0.1) %>%
  as.data.frame.matrix() %>%
  calc_perc_df()

# 1. Transform data to long format
df_long <- df[1:5, ] %>%
  rownames_to_column("Sample") %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = -Sample, 
               names_to = "Cluster", 
               values_to = "Percentage")

# 2. Create the plot
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Percentage, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    # 1. Remove the legend
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # 2. Remove X-axis title
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    
    # 3. Remove X-axis text (labels) and ticks
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    
    # Optional: Styling the Y-axis to match your previous plot
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)")

ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_1_A_ctcomposition.pdf"),
       width = 2.5,
       height = 2.5)
```


## Normalization

```{r}

layer <- "L1"
hist(dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts %>% unlist(), breaks=50)
hist(dproc[[layer]]$freq %>% unlist() * 100, breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_all %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_0.1perc %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")

layer <- "L2"
hist(dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts %>% unlist(), breaks=50)
hist(dproc[[layer]]$freq %>% unlist() * 100, breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_all %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_0.1perc %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")



layer <- "L3"
hist(dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts %>% unlist(), breaks=50)
hist(dproc[[layer]]$freq %>% unlist() * 100, breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_all %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")
hist(dproc[[layer]]$clr_0.1perc %>% unlist(), breaks=50, main="", xlab="")

df <- dproc[[layer]]$cell_counts %>% unlist() %>% as.data.frame()
p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(.))+
  geom_histogram(color="darkblue", fill="lightblue", bins = 15) +
  xlab("Counts") + ylab("") + ggtitle("Cell counts") +
  theme_classic() +
  scale_x_continuous(trans='sqrt') +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), axis.ticks.x=element_blank(), axis.text.y=element_blank(), axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

df <- dproc[[layer]]$freq %>% unlist() %>% as.data.frame() %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~ . * 100))
p2 <- ggplot(df, aes(.))+
  geom_histogram(color="darkblue", fill="lightblue", bins = 15) +
  xlab("%") + ylab("") + ggtitle("Frequency") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), axis.ticks.x=element_blank(), axis.text.y=element_blank(), axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

df <- dproc[[layer]]$clr_1count_all %>% unlist() %>% as.data.frame()
p3 <- ggplot(df, aes(.))+
  geom_histogram(color="darkblue", fill="lightblue", bins = 15) +
  xlab("CLR") + ylab("") + ggtitle("Centered\nlogratio") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x=element_blank(), axis.ticks.x=element_blank(), axis.text.y=element_blank(), axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

patchwork::wrap_plots(p2, p3, nrow = 1)

ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_1_A_normalization_example.pdf"),
       width = 3,
       height = 2.5)
```



# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Plot number of samples and cell types lost per granularity

```{r}
# Apply function to each dataframe
df1 <- dproc[["L1"]][["cell_counts"]]
df2 <- dproc[["L2"]][["cell_counts"]]
df3 <- dproc[["L3"]][["cell_counts"]]

# Assume df1, df2, df3 are your three dataframes
dfs <- list(Low = df1, Medium = df2, High = df3)


# Function to calculate percentage of rows/columns with at least one zero
compute_zero_percentage <- function(df, granularity) {
  total_samples <- nrow(df)
  total_celltypes <- ncol(df)
  
  samples_with_zeros <- sum(rowSums(df == 0) > 0) / total_samples * 100
  celltypes_with_zeros <- sum(colSums(df == 0) > 0) / total_celltypes * 100
  total_zeros <- sum(df == 0) / (nrow(df) * ncol(df)) * 100
  
  granularity_labels <- paste0(granularity, "\n(", total_celltypes, " cell types)")
  
  data.frame(
    Granularity = factor(granularity_labels, levels = granularity_labels),
    Category = c("Samples", "Cell Types", "Total"),
    Percentage = c(samples_with_zeros, celltypes_with_zeros, total_zeros)
  )
}

# Compute percentages for all dataframes
zero_percentages <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(dfs), function(n) compute_zero_percentage(dfs[[n]], n))
)

# Plot
ggplot(zero_percentages, aes(x = Granularity, y = Percentage, group = Category, color = Category, label = round(Percentage, 1))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(vjust=-1, show.legend  = F) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "",
       x = "Granularity",
       y = "Percentage (%)",
       color = "Number of zeros") +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 14)) +
  ylim(0,100)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "coda_numberzeros.pdf"), width = 6, height = 4)
```




# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Figure 1 B: Based on cell type composition


```{r}
feat_mat <- dproc$L3$clr_1count

# Create a mapping vector: "Old Name" = "New Name"
cell_map <- c(
  "SOX4+_naive_CD4_T_cell"          = "CD4+ Naive",
  "SOX4+_naive_CD8_T_cell"          = "CD8+ Naive",
  "Adaptive_NK_cell"                = "NK Adaptive",
  "KLRF1-_GZMB+_CD27-_EM_CD8_T_cell" = "CD8+ EM",
  "KLRF1+_effector_Vd1_gdT"         = "Vd1 Effector",
  # "KLRF1+_effector_Vd1_gdT"         = "Vd1 Effector (KLRF1+)",
  # "KLRF1-_effector_Vd1_gdT"         = "Vd1 Effector (KLRF1-)",
  "KLRF1-_GZMB+_CD27-_memory_CD4_T_cell" = "CD4+ Memory",
  "Naive_Vd1_gdT"                   = "Vd1 Naive",
  "SOX4+_Vd1_gdT"                   = "Vd1 SOX4+",
  "CD8aa"                           = "CD8aa"
)

# Apply to your feature matrix
colnames(feat_mat) <- ifelse(colnames(feat_mat) %in% names(cell_map), 
                             cell_map[colnames(feat_mat)], 
                             colnames(feat_mat))

res.pca <- prcomp(feat_mat)


feat_mat <- res.pca$x[, 1:3]


set.seed(1)
labels <- factor(kmeans(feat_mat, 4)$cluster)


levels(labels) <- c("2", "3", "4", "1")
labels <- factor(labels, levels = c("1", "2", "3", "4"))

my_colors <- c(
  "1" = "#F8766D",
  "2" = "#7CAE00",
  "3" = "#00BFC4",
  "4" = "#C77CFF",
  "CMV- Old" = "#F8766D",
  "CMV+ Old" = "#7CAE00",
  "CMV- Young" = "#00BFC4",
  "CMV+ Young" = "#C77CFF"
)

eig.val <- factoextra::get_eig(res.pca)
pc1_var <- round(eig.val[1, 2], 1)
pc2_var <- round(eig.val[2, 2], 1)


p1 <- factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
                     # habillage = labels,
                     # addEllipses = TRUE,
                     # label = "var",
                     col.ind = "grey80",
                     pointsize = 1,
                     labelsize = 3,
                     invisible = c("quali"),
                     geom = "point",
                     select.var = list(contrib = 4),
                     repel = TRUE,
                     axes.linetype = "blank") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
  guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Cluster"), color=guide_legend(title="Cluster")) +
  ggtitle("PCA - ECODA sample representation") +
  # scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)"))  #+
  # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
  #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
print(p1)

p2 <-factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
                     habillage = dproc$meta$combined_cmv_age,
                     # addEllipses = TRUE,
                     label = "var",
                     pointsize = 1,
                     invisible = c("quali"),
                     col.var = "white",
                     arrowsize = 0,
                     labelsize = 0,
                     alpha.var = 0,
                     geom = "point",
                     select.var = list(contrib = 4),
                     axes.linetype = "blank") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
  guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Bio group"), color=guide_legend(title="Bio group")) +
  ggtitle("Known biological groups") +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)"))  #+
  # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
  #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
print(p2)
```


```{r}
# 1. Run PCA
res.pca <- prcomp(feat_mat, center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)

# 2. Get the eigenvalues (variances)
# prcomp returns standard deviations; square them to get eigenvalues
eig <- (res.pca$sdev)^2

# 3. Calculate Variable Coordinates (Correlations)
# Formula: Loading * Standard Deviation of the PC
# This transforms the rotation matrix into the 'coordinates' used in biplots
variable_coords <- t(t(res.pca$rotation) * res.pca$sdev)

# 4. Calculate Squared Cosines (Quality of representation)
# For variables on a correlation PCA, this is just the squared coordinate
var_cos2 <- variable_coords^2

# 5. Calculate Contributions
# Formula: (cos2 / eigenvalue) * 100
# We divide each column of squared coordinates by its eigenvalue
var_contrib <- as.data.frame(t(t(var_cos2) / eig) * 100)

# Check: The sum of any column in var_contrib should be 100
colSums(var_contrib)


library(factoextra)
res.var <- get_pca_var(res.pca)

max(var_contrib - res.var$contrib)
```

# Figure 1 B: Based on pseudobulk

```{r}
pseudobulk <- readRDS(file.path(path_data, "all_bulk_sample.rds"))
meta <- readRDS(file.path(path_data, "all_bulk_meta.rds"))
meta <- meta %>%
  arrange((specimen.specimenGuid))
meta$age <- ifelse(as.numeric(as.character(meta$sample.subjectAgeAtDraw)) < 50, "young", "old")

pb_normed <- DESeq2.normalize(t(as.data.frame(pseudobulk)[, 2:ncol(pseudobulk)]), meta)
pb_normed <- t(pb_normed)
row.names(pb_normed) <- pseudobulk$specimen.specimenGuid

res.pca <- prcomp(pb_normed, scale. = FALSE)

eig.val <- factoextra::get_eig(res.pca)
pc1_var <- round(eig.val[1, 2], 1)
pc2_var <- round(eig.val[2, 2], 1)

set.seed(1)
labels <- factor(kmeans(pb_normed, 2)$cluster)

levels(labels) <- c("2", "1") # Swap these if the colors are inverted
labels <- factor(labels, levels = c("1", "2")) # Force order 1 then 2

# Ensure these are in your my_colors
my_colors <- c(
  "1" = "orange",
  "2" = "skyblue",
  "Female" = "orange",
  "Male" = "skyblue"
)

p3 <- factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
                     # habillage = labels,
                     # addEllipses = TRUE,
                     col.ind = "grey80",
                     label = "var",
                     pointsize = 1,
                     invisible = c("quali"),
                     geom = "point",
                     select.var = list(contrib = 4),
                     labelsize = 3,
                     repel = TRUE,
                     axes.linetype = "blank") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
  guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Cluster"), color=guide_legend(title="Cluster")) +
  ggtitle("PCA - Pseudobulk sample representation") +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) #+
  # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
  #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
p3 <- p3 + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 20)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-40, 40, by = 20))
print(p3)

labels <- meta$subject.biologicalSex

p4 <-factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
                     habillage = labels,
                     # addEllipses = TRUE,
                     label = "var",
                     pointsize = 1,
                     invisible = c("quali"),
                     col.var = "white",
                     arrowsize = 0,
                     labelsize = 0,
                     alpha.var = 0,
                     select.var = list(contrib = 4),
                     geom = "point",
                     axes.linetype = "blank") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
  guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Bio group"), color=guide_legend(title="Bio group")) +
  ggtitle("Known biological groups") +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + coord_equal() +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) #+
  # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
  #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
p4 <- p4 + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 20)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-40, 40, by = 20))
print(p4)


# p5 <- factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
#                      habillage = labels,
#                      # addEllipses = TRUE,
#                      # label = "var",
#                      pointsize = 1,
#                      invisible = c("quali", "var"),
#                      geom = "point",
#                      # select.var = list(contrib = 4),
#                      labelsize = 3,
#                      repel = TRUE,
#                      axes.linetype = "blank") +
#     scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
#   guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Cluster"), color=guide_legend(title="Cluster")) +
#   ggtitle("Unsupervised clustering") +
#   scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + coord_equal() +
#   theme_classic() #+
#   # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
#   #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
# p5 <- p5 + 
#   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 20)) + 
#   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-20, 20, by = 20))
# print(p5)
# 
# labels <- meta$subject.biologicalSex
# 
# p6 <-factoextra::fviz_pca(res.pca,
#                      habillage = labels,
#                      # addEllipses = TRUE,
#                      # label = "var",
#                      pointsize = 1,
#                      invisible = c("quali", "var"),
#                      col.var = "white",
#                      arrowsize = 0,
#                      labelsize = 0,
#                      alpha.var = 0,
#                      # select.var = list(contrib = 4),
#                      geom = "point",
#                      axes.linetype = "blank") +
#     scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
#   guides(shape=guide_legend(title="Bio group"), color=guide_legend(title="Bio group")) +
#   ggtitle("Known biological groups") +
#   scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) + coord_equal() +
#   theme_classic() #+
#   # theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
#   #         axis.ticks = element_blank())
# p6 <- p6 + 
#   scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-60, 60, by = 20)) + 
#   scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(-20, 20, by = 20))
# print(p6)

aligned_plots <- p1 + p2 + p3 + p4 + plot_layout(ncol = 2)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_1_B_GongSharma_clustering.pdf"), aligned_plots, width = 8, height = 6)

# aligned_plots <- p1 + p2 + p3 + p4 + p5 + p6 + plot_layout(ncol = 2)
# ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "TEST.pdf"), aligned_plots, width = 8, height = 8)
```



# Supp Fig 1: GongSharma PB per sex

```{r}
plot_list <- list()

for (gender in unique(meta$subject.biologicalSex)) {
  pb_normed_sub <- pb_normed[meta$subject.biologicalSex == gender, ]
  meta_sub <- meta[meta$subject.biologicalSex == gender]
  
  res.pca_sub <- prcomp(pb_normed_sub, scale. = FALSE)
  
  eig.val <- factoextra::get_eig(res.pca_sub)
  pc1_var <- round(eig.val[1, 2], 1)
  pc2_var <- round(eig.val[2, 2], 1)
  
  labels <- meta_sub$subject.cmv
  condition <- "CMV"
  title <- "Biological variable 1"
  plot_list[[paste0(gender, "_", condition)]] <- factoextra::fviz_pca(
    res.pca_sub,
    habillage = labels,
    # addEllipses = TRUE,
    label = "none",
    pointsize = 1,
    invisible = c("var", "quali"),
    # repel = TRUE,
    geom = "point",
    axes.linetype = NA) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
    guides(shape=guide_legend(title=condition), color=guide_legend(title=condition)) +
    ggtitle(paste(gender, "-", condition)) +
    labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(# axis.title.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      # axis.title.y=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank()) + coord_equal()
  print(plot_list[[paste0(gender, "_", condition)]])
  
  labels <- meta_sub$age
  condition <- "Age"
  plot_list[[paste0(gender, "_", condition)]] <- factoextra::fviz_pca(
    res.pca_sub,
    habillage = labels,
    # addEllipses = TRUE,
    label = "none",
    pointsize = 1,
    invisible = c("var", "quali"),
    # repel = TRUE,
    geom = "point",
    axes.linetype = NA) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = rep(19, length(unique(labels)))) +
    guides(shape=guide_legend(title=condition), color=guide_legend(title=condition)) +
    ggtitle(paste(gender, "-", condition)) +
    labs(x = paste0("PCA dim1 (", pc1_var, "%)"), y = paste0("PCA dim2 (", pc2_var, "%)")) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(# axis.title.x=element_blank(),
      axis.text.x=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x=element_blank(),
      # axis.title.y=element_blank(),
      axis.text.y=element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.y=element_blank()) + coord_equal()
  print(plot_list[[paste0(gender, "_", condition)]])
}

patchwork::wrap_plots(plot_list, nrow = 2)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_1_GongSharma_PB_CMV_Age.pdf"), width = 7, height = 5)
```


### Heatmp (ct comp)

```{r fig.height=12, fig.width=12}

clust_labels <- cluster::pam(feat_mat_females, k = 4)$cluster

get_label <- function(string) {
  if (grepl("Immune Variation|Stand|Day 90", string)) {
    return("Baseline")
  } else {
    match <- "Vax"
    # match <- regmatches(string, regexpr("Day \\d+", string))
    return(match)
  }
}
meta$flu_vax_day <- as.character(meta$sample.visitName)
meta$flu_vax_day <- sapply(meta$flu_vax_day, get_label)

df_heatmap <- feat_mat_females %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
df_heatmap <- df_heatmap %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  Immune_type = clust_labels,
  CMV = meta_females$subject.cmv,
  Age = meta_females$age,
  BMI = ifelse(na.aggregate(as.numeric(as.character(meta_females$subject.bmi)), FUN = mean) >30, ">30", "<30")
) %>% as.data.frame()

pheatmap(df_heatmap, annotation_col = my_sample_col, scale = "none", clustering_method = "ward.D2")


df1 <- feat_mat_females
df1 <- df1[ , sqrt(colVars(feat_mat_females)) > 0.5]
df1 %>%
  prcomp() %>%
  p_pca(labels = meta_females$subject.cmv)
df1 %>%
  prcomp() %>%
  p_pca(labels = meta_females$age)

df_heatmap <- df1 %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
df_heatmap <- df_heatmap %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  Immune_type = clust_labels,
  CMV = meta_females$subject.cmv,
  Age = meta_females$age
) %>% as.data.frame()

pheatmap(df_heatmap, annotation_col = my_sample_col, scale = "none", clustering_method = "ward.D2")


feat_mat <- dproc[["L3"]]$clr_0.1perc
plot(sqrt(colVars(feat_mat)))

df1 <- feat_mat

dist_mat <- dist(df1)
hc <- stats::hclust(dist_mat, method = "ward.D2")
clust_labels <- stats::cutree(hc, k = 4)
# clust_labels <- cluster::pam(df1, k = 4)$cluster

clust_labels_reordered <- clust_labels
clust_labels_reordered[clust_labels == 4] <- 1
clust_labels_reordered[clust_labels == 1] <- 2
clust_labels_reordered[clust_labels == 2] <- 4
clust_labels_reordered <- as.factor(clust_labels_reordered)

gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
  hues = seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
  hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
}
gg_colors <- gg_color_hue(4)

ann_colors = list(
    # Vaccination_day = c(Vax = "red", Baseline = "black")
  # BMI = c(Adipose = "red", Lean = "black")
  Immune_type = c("1" = gg_colors[1], "2" = gg_colors[2], "3" = gg_colors[3], "4" = gg_colors[4])
)

df_heatmap <- df1 %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
df_heatmap <- df_heatmap %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  # Race = meta$subject.race,
  # BMI = ifelse(na.aggregate(as.numeric(as.character(meta$subject.bmi)), FUN = mean) >30, "Adipose", "Lean"),
  # Vaccination_day = meta$flu_vax_day,
  Age = meta$age,
  CMV = meta$subject.cmv,
  Immune_type = clust_labels_reordered
) %>% as.data.frame()

p <- pheatmap(
  df_heatmap,
  annotation_col = my_sample_col,
  scale = "none",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  treeheight_row = 0,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cutree_rows = 4,
  cutree_cols = 4,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors
  )
pdf(file.path(path_plots, "heatmap_gongsharma_ctcomp_full.pdf"), width=10, height=10)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()



df_heatmap <- df1 %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
df_heatmap <- df_heatmap %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  # Race = meta$subject.race,
  # BMI = ifelse(na.aggregate(as.numeric(as.character(meta$subject.bmi)), FUN = mean) >30, "Adipose", "Lean"),
  # Vaccination_day = meta$flu_vax_day,
  Age = meta$age,
  CMV = meta$subject.cmv,
  Immune_type = clust_labels_reordered
) %>% as.data.frame()

p <- pheatmap(
  df_heatmap,
  annotation_col = my_sample_col,
  scale = "none",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  # treeheight_row = 0,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cutree_rows = 4,
  cutree_cols = 4 ,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors
  )
pdf(file.path(path_plots, "heatmap_gongsharma_ctcomp_full_withrowclustering.pdf"), width=10, height=10)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()



row_order <- p[["tree_row"]][["order"]]
col_order <- p[["tree_col"]][["order"]]
cts <- p[["tree_row"]][["labels"]][row_order]


df1 <- feat_mat[col_order, cts[1:17]]
# df1 <- df1[ , sqrt(colVars(feat_mat)) > 0.5]


df1 %>%
  prcomp() %>%
  p_pca(labels = meta$subject.cmv[col_order])
df1 %>%
  prcomp() %>%
  p_pca(labels = meta$age[col_order])

df_heatmap <- df1 %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE)
df_heatmap <- df_heatmap %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  Immune_type = clust_labels,
  CMV = meta$subject.cmv,
  Age = meta$age #,
  # Sex = meta$subject.biologicalSex
) %>% as.data.frame()

p <- pheatmap(
  df_heatmap,
  annotation_col = my_sample_col,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  scale = "none",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cutree_rows = 3,
  cutree_cols = 4)
pdf(file.path(path_plots, "heatmap_gongsharma_ctcomp_subset_hvc.pdf"), width=10, height=5)
grid::grid.newpage()
grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
dev.off()

```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Test of PCA scaling (factoextra vs vegan)

```{r}
library(vegan)
feat_mat <- dproc$L3$clr_1count
x <- vegan::pca(feat_mat)
biplot(x, scaling = "none")
biplot(x, scaling = "species")
biplot(x, scaling = "site")


# 2. Extract scores for different scalings
# 'sites' refers to your samples/rows
s1 <- as.data.frame(scores(x, display = "sites", scaling = "none"))
s2 <- as.data.frame(scores(x, display = "sites", scaling = "sites"))
s3 <- as.data.frame(scores(x, display = "sites", scaling = "species"))

s1 <- s1 * 15
s2 <- s2 * 2.3
s3 <- s3 * 1.045

# 3. Add a label so we can tell them apart
s1$type <- "none"
s2$type <- "sites"
s3$type <- "species"

# 4. Combine and Plot
all_scales <- rbind(s1, s2, s3)

ggplot(all_scales, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Overlay of PCA Scalings",
       subtitle = "If points overlap perfectly, the scaling didn't shift their relative positions")

# 1. Generate the factoextra plot object
# (Assuming 'res.pca' is your prcomp or FactoMineR object)
p_fviz <- fviz_pca_ind(res.pca, geom = "point")

# 2. Extract the coordinates factoextra calculated
# These are stored in p_fviz$data
fviz_points <- p_fviz$data[, c("x", "y")] /6.2
colnames(fviz_points) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
fviz_points$type <- "factoextra_default"

# 3. Combine with your existing data
all_scales_plus_fviz <- rbind(all_scales, fviz_points)

# 4. Plot everything together
ggplot(all_scales_plus_fviz, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("none" = "black", 
                                "sites" = "blue", 
                                "species" = "green", 
                                "factoextra_default" = "red")) +
  labs(title = "Overlay: Vegan Scalings vs. Factoextra",
       subtitle = "Note: Red points show the factoextra default 'Symmetric' scaling")
```

```{r}
# Update your plot code with these specific shapes
ggplot(all_scales_plus_fviz, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = type, shape = type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, stroke = 1) +  # Stroke makes the lines thicker
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(
    "none" = 1,               # Hollow circle
    "sites" = 3,              # Plus sign
    "species" = 4,            # Cross (X)
    "factoextra_default" = 21 # Filled circle (use a different size for this)
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "none" = "black", 
    "sites" = "blue", 
    "species" = "green3", 
    "factoextra_default" = "red"
  ))
```

```{r}
ggplot(all_scales_plus_fviz, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = type)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~type) + # This creates 4 separate plots
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Faceted Comparison of Scalings")
```



# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Blood from Terekhova et al.

Single-cell atlas of healthy human blood unveils age-related loss of NKG2C+GZMB−CD8+ memory T cells and accumulation of type 2 memory T cells
Marina Terekhova1,6 ∙ Amanda Swain1,6 ∙ Pavla Bohacova1,6 ∙ Ekaterina Aladyeva1 ∙ Laura Arthur1 ∙ Anwesha Laha1 ∙ Denis A. Mogilenko1,2 ∙ Samantha Burdess1 ∙ Vladimir Sukhov1,3 ∙ Denis Kleverov1,3 ∙ Barbora Echalar1 ∙ Petr Tsurinov1,4 ∙ Roman Chernyatchik1,5 ∙ Kamila Husarcikova1 ∙ Maxim N. Artyomov1,7 martyomov@wustl.edu Show less

Data from:
https://www.cell.com/immunity/fulltext/S1074-7613(23)00453-3?_returnURL=https%3A%2F%2Flinkinghub.elsevier.com%2Fretrieve%2Fpii%2FS1074761323004533%3Fshowall%3Dtrue



## Load data

```{r}
library(readxl)

df_ter <- data.frame(read_xlsx(file.path(getwd(), "data", "other", "TerekhovaM_2023_CellImmu", "mmc7.xlsx")))
meta_ter <- data.frame(read_xlsx(file.path(getwd(), "data", "other", "TerekhovaM_2023_CellImmu", "mmc2.xlsx")))

df_ter <- df_ter[order(df_ter$Donor_id), ]
meta_ter <- meta_ter[order(meta_ter$Donor_id), ]

identical(df_ter$Donor_id, meta_ter$Donor_id)

meta_ter <- meta_ter[, c("Donor_id", "Gender", "Ethnicity", "Age_group")]
meta_ter$Age <- df_ter$Age
meta_ter$BMI <- df_ter$BMI
meta_ter$Age_bin <- ifelse(meta_ter$Age > 50, ">50", "<=50")

df_ter_lowres <- df_ter[, 8:16]

df_ter[, 17:20] <- df_ter[, 17:20] * df_ter_lowres$Myeloid.cells / 100
df_ter[, 21:36] <- df_ter[, 21:36] * df_ter_lowres$CD4..T.cells / 100
df_ter[, 37:48] <- df_ter[, 37:48] * df_ter_lowres$TRAV1.2...CD8..T.cells / 100
df_ter[, 49:54] <- df_ter[, 49:54] * df_ter_lowres$NK.cells / 100
df_ter[, 55:59] <- df_ter[, 55:59] * df_ter_lowres$gd.T.cells / 100
df_ter[, 60:68] <- df_ter[, 60:68] * df_ter_lowres$B.cells / 100
df_ter_hires <- df_ter[, c(10, 12, 16, 17:(ncol(df_ter)-2))]


```


```{r fig.height=9, fig.width=9}
feat_mat <- df_ter_lowres %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "percentage_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.1) %>%
  clr()
p <- plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = meta_ter$Age_group)

feat_mat <- df_ter_hires %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "percentage_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.1) %>%
  clr()
p <- plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = meta_ter$Age_group)
p <- plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = meta_ter$Age_group, invisible = "var", labelsize = 4)
p <- plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = meta_ter$Age_group, invisible = "none", labelsize = 4)

p <- plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = meta_ter$Age_bin, plotly_3d = TRUE)


p <- plot_pca(feat_mat[meta_ter$Age_group %in% c("A", "D", "E"), ], labels = meta_ter$Age_group[meta_ter$Age_group %in% c("A", "D", "E")])
p <- plot_pca(feat_mat[meta_ter$Age_group %in% c("A", "E"), ], labels = meta_ter$Age_group[meta_ter$Age_group %in% c("A", "E")], invisible = "none", labelsize = 4)

```
